title: ISic003626. Epitaph for Agathe
body:
Text encoding: Jonathan Prag; edition source: https://www.zotero.org/groups/382445/items/8ENX9UZ5
1𐆠
2ἐτελεύτησε[ν]
3Ἀγάθη χρησ-
4τιανὴ μαίῳ
5μηνὶ τς ε´
6εἰσβατό[ς]

Text encoding: Jonathan Prag; edition source: https://www.zotero.org/groups/382445/items/8ENX9UZ5
1𐆠
2ΕΤΕΛΕΥΤΗΣΕ[.]
3ΑΓΑΘΗΧΡΗΣ
4ΤΙΑΝΗΜΑΙΩ
5ΜΗΝΙΤΣΕ
6ΕΙΣΒΑΤΟ[.]

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

translation

((Staurogram)) Agathe, a Christian, passed away the fifth day of May, accessible

commentary

It is unclear what the final word might signify, whether it is meant to indicate something like 'welcoming' (so Orsi), or instead to indicate that further burials are permitted in the loculus (so Rizzone). Ferrua proposed reading εἰς βᾶκε, i.e. 'in pace', 'in peace'.

bibliography

  • Orsi 1934 151-152 no.3 fig.22 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001697
  • Orsi 1942 225-226 tav.17 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001696
  • Ferrua 1943-1944 98-99 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10002000
  • Rizzone 2009 55-56 no.5 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001633

Alpha tending to cursive, epsilon quadrate with equal bars, sigma lunate of quadrate form, theta ovoid with full cross-bar.

Line 1-6 25 Interlineation line 1 to 2
